title: lithocardium subpubescens
intro: Lithocardium subpubescens, also known as Digitalis purpurea, is a flowering plant in the foxglove family native to Europe. The medicinal properties of Lithocardium subpubescens are derived from its leaves, which contain digoxin and digitoxin, cardiac glycosides that have been used to treat heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure. The health benefits of Lithocardium subpubescens include improved heart function, reduced symptoms of heart failure, and increased survival rates in patients with certain heart conditions. Lithocardium subpubescens can be prepared as a tincture, capsule, or tablet for oral administration, and is often used in herbal medicine to treat heart-related disorders.
